ELPAC Level 2
Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Who Gets the Microphone

STS-MS8-L2-10001300-MSSS8-TSP-NAV-02 • MS Social Studies Grade 8 • Reconstruction
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
A panel show puts different voices at one desk so the audience hears more than one side. Marcus, Ruby, and Tobias are broadcasting a town hall about what freedom would mean after the Civil War. Three amendments tried to answer that question: the 13th ended slavery, the 14th made citizens, and the 15th protected the vote. Newspapers carried the argument into homes across the country, and in 1870 Hiram Revels became the first Black United States senator.
Which of the three amendments do you think changed daily life the fastest, and why?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Marcus doing?
Look at the picture. Describe what Marcus is doing in one full sentence.
2
What is Ruby holding?
Look at the picture. Describe what Ruby is holding in one full sentence.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Describe what the teacher is doing in one full sentence.
4
Where are they? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Describe this place in one full sentence.
5
Tell me about the board behind them.
Look at the picture. Describe the wall behind them in one full sentence.
6
What is Tobias wearing?
Look at the picture. Describe what Tobias is wearing in one full sentence.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CA HSS 8.11 — Reconstruction and the 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
